_moveSpeedComment: PLAYER RUN SPEED in m/s (B4, 2026-07-18, VRmike): the single authoritative movement speed for EVERY player. Raised +50% from 6 to 9 for playtest feel. Read from THIS one knob by both the host's authoritative movement (shared/referee.js integrate + shared/physics.js _substep collide-and-slide) AND every client's own prediction, so raising it speeds everyone up in lockstep — no desync, and no movement sanity-check to update (movement is host-authoritative from input INTENT, not client-reported positions; there is no 'moving too fast' guard to fool). HOT-TUNABLE playtest value, NOT sacred — VRmike expects to retune it (may be too zoomy in the restaurant's tight corridors; dial back here, one number, no rebuild). See notes/balance-tuning.md.
_maxDynamicPropsComment: PHONE-SAFE dynamic-body budget. Everything-is-physics (2026-07-16) made the built-ins real dynamic bodies, so the restaurant has ~239 pre-removal / ~180 post-removal should-be-dynamic candidates. The referee sorts them GLOBALLY biggest-first; physics._buildProps gives the first `maxDynamicProps` a real rigid body and the rest degrade to a still-collidable but IMMOVABLE static collider. PHYSICS SETTLE ROUND 5 (2026-07-17, VRmike): the old 150 cap left the ~89 SMALLEST candidates (all 16 ketchup + 14 mustard sauce bottles, 13 beige canisters, 2 paper-towel rolls, 20 stools, plates + food scraps) as static colliders that REFUSE TO FALL EVEN WHEN HIT — exactly VRmike's 'the cylinders outright refuse' bug. VRmike's standing rule is everything non-architecture is dynamic and falls, so the cap is raised to 260 (> the 248 promoted / 239 dynamic candidates) so NOTHING a player can knock over is silently demoted to immovable. tools/check-settle.mjs FAILS the build if any should-be-dynamic prop is left without a dynamic body. Correctness over the match-start settle hitch (VRmike pre-approved it). Aggressive Rapier auto-sleep keeps steady-state cost near zero once settled. PHONE HOSTS are the watch item — HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild): if a warm phone host chugs too hard at match start, lower it (accepting the smallest scraps become unshovable again) or bake pre-settled maps. See notes/physics.md + notes/map-randomization.md.
_pinClutterRemoved: FLOATING-FIXED-PROPS ROUND 4 (2026-07-17, VRmike): the `pinClutterAboveY` pin is GONE. It froze every surface prop authored above 0.5m (plates/food/dishes/condiments) as a FIXED collider hanging at its authored height — you could stand on them and they jittered nearby dynamic bodies. VRmike's standing instruction is EVERYTHING non-architecture is dynamic and falls; only wall/architecture-attached pieces (doors/vents/pillars, flagged `wallAttached`) stay fixed. The launch-out-of-a-tall-hull problem the pin worked around is solved at the source: shared/grounding.js seatMapData seats each item on the collider actually beneath it at load, so nothing spawns interpenetrating. tools/check-floating-props.mjs FAILS the build if a pin (or any non-architecture fixed body) ever comes back.
_propWeightComment: SIZE-BASED PROP WEIGHT + KNOCKABILITY (2026-07-19, VRmike). Every dynamic prop's MASS is propDensity × its collider VOLUME — and because volume grows with size³, a prop twice as tall is ~8× heavier (the cubic real-world density relationship VRmike asked for). Rapier bakes this straight from setDensity(propDensity) in shared/physics.js, so it needs no per-prop authoring and updates automatically when a prop is (re)built from a different disguise footprint. propDensity is THE single heaviness dial: raise it and EVERY prop gets heavier (harder for a hunter to shove/fling, small props included), lower it and everything lightens. Big disguises (fridge/table) are naturally sluggish, small ones (burger/tomato) easy to yeet. The never-immovable guarantee is the SEPARATE minNudgeSpeed floor below. No mass floor/ceiling clamps are used — Rapier's own tiny-mass guard is the only hidden safety, kept out of the balance surface so the light end stays lively. Hunters are UNTOUCHED (characterMass stays as-is). HOT-TUNABLE. See notes/balance-tuning.md.
_minNudgeSpeedComment: SIZE-BASED PROP WEIGHT — MINIMUM NUDGE (2026-07-19, VRmike). The minimum velocity change (m/s) a prop is GUARANTEED from any discrete hit (rifle shot or grenade fling), even one its mass would otherwise swallow almost entirely — VRmike's 'even a fridge can't be immovable, it must ALWAYS be slightly nudgeable'. Both discrete-impulse sources route through shared/physics.js _nudgeImpulseMag, so this ONE floor covers them; it never exceeds the hit's own intended speed, so a weak far-edge grenade shove is not amplified into a launch. Dial this UP to make huge props more nudgeable, DOWN for more stubborn heavies. HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild). The continuous walk-into-a-prop shove is naturally mass-scaled by Rapier and always imparts some motion, so it needs no floor. See notes/balance-tuning.md.
_heavyNudgeComment: SOLID DISGUISED PLAYERS (2026-07-18, Jie): a disguised prop-player is a KINEMATIC body — infinite mass, so a shove that slides a REAL dynamic prop stops dead against the disguised one (the tell). heavyNudgeSpeed (m/s) is the SLOW capped crawl a SUSTAINED push slides a disguised player at — like shoving heavy furniture, no tip (kinematic bodies never rotate on their own). Keep it well under moveSpeed (6) so a disguised player can be inched, never flung, and can't be abused to teleport (host-authoritative, resolved through the pushed player's own controller so it collide-and-slides vs walls). heavyNudgeContactSkin (m) is how far past the pusher's own collider it detects a press. heavyNudgeWarmupFrames is how many consecutive contact substeps before the crawl starts (a glancing tap barely moves them). Undisguised players are NEVER nudged — hunter-vs-hunter / general player-vs-player is untouched. All HOT-TUNABLE. See notes/solid-disguised-players.md.
_spawnOverlapComment: SEAM D3 (2026-07-18): spawnOverlapPushMax is the max metres resolveSpawnOverlap may slide a freshly spawned/teleported player to clear another player it materialised inside (shared hunter spawn, team-switch respawn, mid-join). Host-authoritative, wall-clamped, iteration-bounded — nobody spawns fused inside anyone. B2 (2026-07-18) extends the SAME resolver to also lift/nudge a spawn out of any PROP or interior static fixture it settled inside (settled-prop embedding + a stagger that ended inside a pillar), reusing the per-substep prop depenetration. HOT-TUNABLE.
_leaveTimeoutComment: CONNECTION LIVENESS VIA KEEPALIVE PINGS (2026-07-19, VRmike): the threshold — in SECONDS — of total connection silence before a peer is treated as GENUINELY disconnected. ONE knob drives BOTH directions: the host's silent-player sweep (referee._sweepSilentPlayers removes a guest gone quiet this long) AND the guest's host watchdog (main.js hostSilenceMs → boots to the lobby when the host goes quiet this long). Liveness is judged by the LAST MESSAGE OF ANY KIND — a dedicated ~1Hz keepalive ping (both directions, net.js) OR any C2S/snapshot — NEVER by input specifically, so an AFK-but-connected player (someone idle in the bathroom) is indistinguishable from an active one and is NEVER booted. Raised 5→15s: a backgrounded browser tab holding a live WebRTC connection still ticks setInterval at ~1Hz but with multi-second jitter spikes (see notes/disconnect-diagnosis.md), so a tight window false-positives on a throttled-but-alive phone. 15s tolerates the jitter — only a truly dead connection (tab closed, phone asleep past the wake-lock, network drop; pings stop entirely) crosses the line. HOT-TUNABLE. The host's own loopback player is never swept (the referee lives in its tab).
_voteKickComment: VOTE-KICK (2026-07-19, VRmike): player-driven removal of an AFK/problem player — the human replacement for the retired automatic AFK-boot. voteKickSeconds is the countdown (in SECONDS) a vote-kick runs before it resolves on the timer; it resolves EARLY the moment every eligible voter has cast. voteKickCooldownSeconds is the PER-TARGET anti-spam cooldown after a FAILED vote — a new vote-kick against THAT SAME player is refused host-side for this long (only the just-survived target is penalised; everyone else is votable the instant the vote ends). Both HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild). Host-authoritative: ONE vote game-wide at a time, majority YES of votes CAST kicks (tie/majority-no keeps), the initiator is an automatic YES, and the kicked player is removed via the exact same cleanup path as a leaver. See shared/referee.js startVoteKick + notes/vote-kick.md.
_mapRandomizeSkipComment: HIDE-SPOT REMOVAL (2026-07-16, VRmike): fraction of DISGUISABLE scenery deleted at match load to open hiding gaps for prop players. Bumped 0.20 -> 0.25, and (this build) widened to cover EVERYTHING a prop can disguise as — the disguise-pool props (map.props) AND every non-architecture fixture (knockable tables/cookware AND bolted-in built-ins: counters, pillars, fridge, doors). Architecture (floors/walls/ceilings) is never eligible. Same rule (physics.isDisguisableEntry) drives removal and the disguise pool so they can't drift. See referee.startMatch + notes/map-randomization.md.
_shotImpulseComment: SHOT IMPULSE (2026-07-12, VRmike): a shot that lands on a DYNAMIC (knockable) prop gives it a small host-authoritative physics kick at the hit point along the shot direction, so shot items visibly react. This is the TARGET linear speed kick in m/s at the nudge REFERENCE mass. SIZE-BASED WEIGHT (2026-07-19, VRmike): shared/physics.js now routes it through _nudgeImpulseMag so the velocity change scales INVERSELY with the prop's mass — a light burger flies, a heavy table barely scoots (no longer the SAME nudge for both) — with the minNudgeSpeed floor so even a huge prop still budges. Keep it small (a nudge, not a rocket launcher); raise for a punchier feel. HOT-TUNABLE, no rebuild. Host-only; replicates through the normal prop snapshot stream.
_finderComment: PROP FINDER — the hunter's second selectable tool (2026-07-17, VRmike). When selected it renders a large TRANSLUCENT CYLINDER (green ready / grey cooling) centred on the hunter; LEFT-CLICK (PC) / the fire button (mobile) activates it, forcing a random UNCANCELLABLE taunt out of EVERY living prop within finderRadius (2D distance — the cylinder is effectively infinite in height, so height is ignored). finderRadius is METRES; finderCooldownSeconds is a PER-HUNTER, host-enforced cooldown (each hunter counts down independently, never shared). Both are HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild) — VRmike expects to adjust them in testing. See shared/referee.js applyFind + notes/prop-finder.md.
_fireRateComment: RAPID FIRE (2026-07-12, VRmike): the assault rifle is now HOLD-to-fire at a realistic assault-rifle/SMG cadence. fireRateRpm is the single tunable (rounds per minute; 600-800 is the real-world band). The HOST derives its authoritative fire-rate cap from this (60000/rpm ms between shots, minus a small jitter grace so a legit on-time client isn't throttled below the intended rate) so a modified client can't shoot faster; the CLIENT paces its held-fire auto-repeat off the same number. Damage per bullet is UNCHANGED (damage.base = 5%). fireCooldownMs above is the legacy fallback used only if fireRateRpm is absent.
_damageComment: HUNTER-TOOLS v1 damage tuning (all hot-tunable, no rebuild). base = damage a hunter's rifle does to an undisguised player (5 => 20 hits to kill at defaultMult; DAMAGE-TUNING 2026-07-12 lowered this 10->5). PROP HEALTH SCALING — SIZE-COMPARISON FACTOR (2026-07-19, VRmike): a disguised prop-PLAYER scales base by a SIZE multiplier that COMPARES its footprint size to the PLAYER's size, shrunk by sizeComparisonFactor: multiplier = 1 / (propSize / (playerSize * sizeComparisonFactor)) = (playerSize * sizeComparisonFactor) / propSize. propSize (metres) = 2*max half-extent of the disguise's footprint — the SAME size source physics bakes colliders from, so it auto-upgrades to measured bounds. playerSize is the player capsule's longest dimension (2*(playerRadius+playerHalfHeight) = 1.8 m), derived live by the referee so the pivot tracks the real player. The NEUTRAL point (multiplier 1.0 = plain base damage) is a prop whose size == playerSize * sizeComparisonFactor; with the default 0.6 that's 0.6× the player (~1.08 m): a burger (~0.72 m) => ~1.5× (fragile, still dies faster than an undisguised player), a fridge (~1.88 m) => ~0.57× (tanky), a table (~2.25 m) => ~0.48× (tankier). LOWER sizeComparisonFactor => pivot drops => EVERY prop gets tankier (big props gain the most); it's the one knob to nudge if hunts run long/short. The result is CLAMPED to [largeMult, smallMult] guardrails: smallMult is the multiplier CEILING (a prop smaller than pivot/smallMult can't be one-shot-vaporised), largeMult the FLOOR (a huge prop can't become immortal). smallSize/largeSize are NO LONGER read by the damage curve — they now anchor ONLY the prop-'ouch' pitch curve (see _combat sfx), kept here so pitch and size stay in lockstep. defaultMult applies to an UNDISGUISED player (no size). WRONG-GUESS PENALTY (a hunter shooting a disguisable decoy — a prop or non-arch fixture that COULD have been a player) is a FLAT `base` hit with NO size multiplier EVER (20 wrong guesses = dead); real architecture is still a free miss. selfScalesWithSize is retained as false and is NO LONGER read by the referee (the penalty is unconditionally flat).
_grenadeComment: HUNTER GRENADES — the hunter's THIRD selectable tool (2026-07-17, VRmike). LEFT-CLICK (PC) / the fire button (mobile) while the grenade is selected fires a RAYCAST from the aim point into the world; the grenade explodes INSTANTLY at the first hit point (no arc/travel/fuse/cooldown). All three knobs are HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild). baseDamage is a FRACTION of full health (0.45 = 45%); the referee multiplies it by startHealth to get HP, then by the SAME prop-size multiplier the rifle uses (so tiny burger props take proportionally more and die — the whole point). RADIUS + FALLOFF are authored as fullDamageRadius + falloffDistance, NOT as a single outer radius, exactly as VRmike asked (less error-prone to edit): fullDamageRadius = the metres of MAX damage; falloffDistance = the metres ADDED past it over which damage lerps to ~0, so total range = fullDamageRadius + falloffDistance. B3 BALANCE (2026-07-18, VRmike): both knobs scaled ×0.6 (grenade was OP) — 0.6 + 1.2 = 1.8 outer (down from 1 + 2 = 3). d<=0.6m => full (base×sizeMult); 0.6m<d<1.8m => base×sizeMult×(1-(d-0.6)/1.2); d>=1.8m => 0. HOT-TUNABLE — playtest value, not sacred. BACKFIRE: the blast also hurts the THROWING hunter through non-player decoy props (the same 'could be a player but isn't' objects the rifle backfires on) — flat baseDamage×falloff, NO size multiplier, so ~3 direct decoy hits (0.45×3=1.35 of full HP) are lethal WITHOUT hardcoding '3'. NO friendly fire, NO direct self-damage. REDEMPTION: if the blast kills >=1 prop PLAYER the thrower is restored to FULL HP even when backfire would have been lethal (host resolves prop-player damage first, then decides backfire). Host-authoritative — the client only sends its aim origin/direction; the host recomputes the blast point. FLING (2026-07-18, VRmike): every loose DYNAMIC prop caught in the blast also gets an outward physics shove whose TARGET speed (m/s) scales LINEARLY with the grenade damage at its distance (falloff) — flingSpeed is that speed at FULL damage (blast centre), lerping to ~0 at the outer edge, at the nudge REFERENCE mass. SIZE-BASED WEIGHT (2026-07-19, VRmike): routed through shared/physics.js _nudgeImpulseMag so the velocity change scales INVERSELY with the prop's mass — a light burger flies far, a heavy table barely scoots — with the minNudgeSpeed floor so even the biggest prop still budges from a blast (was: SAME nudge for heavy + light). Host-only; rides the normal prop snapshot stream to everyone (no new netcode). Disguised PLAYERS are kinematic and deliberately immune — only loose world objects fly. HOT-TUNABLE (0 disables the fling). See shared/referee.js _resolveGrenadeBlast + physics.applyBlastImpulse. See shared/damage.js grenadeFalloff + shared/referee.js applyGrenade + notes/hunter-grenades.md.
